name: Caucasian Eagle
description: The Caucasian Eagle is a monstrous bird in Greek mythology, primarily known for its role in the punishment of Prometheus. This colossal eagle, a creature of immense size and strength, was tasked by Zeus to torment the Titan Prometheus for defying the gods and gifting fire to humanity. Every day, the eagle would descend upon Prometheus, who was chained to a rock in the Caucasus Mountains, and devour his liver. Because Prometheus was immortal, his liver would regenerate overnight, only for the eagle to return the next day and repeat the agonizing process. This cycle of torture continued for centuries, demonstrating the wrath of Zeus and the immense suffering endured by Prometheus. The eagle is often depicted as a symbol of divine retribution and unrelenting torment. Its existence highlights the power dynamics between gods and Titans, and the consequences of challenging the established order. Eventually, the hero Heracles (Hercules) would slay the eagle, freeing Prometheus from his eternal torment. The Caucasian Eagle stands as a grim reminder of divine justice and the price of defiance.
subtitle: Tormentor of Prometheus, Symbol of Retribution
appearance: The Caucasian Eagle is described as an enormous bird of prey, significantly larger than any ordinary eagle. Its plumage is typically depicted as being dark, perhaps black or brown, adding to its menacing appearance. It possesses razor-sharp talons and a powerful beak capable of tearing flesh with ease. Its eyes are often described as glowing with a malevolent intelligence, reflecting its purpose as an instrument of divine punishment. Its wingspan is immense, allowing it to soar effortlessly through the skies and quickly descend upon its victim.
habitat: The Caucasian Eagle resided in the Caucasus Mountains, specifically in the region where Prometheus was chained to a rock. This remote and desolate location served as the stage for the Titan's eternal torment, emphasizing the isolation and suffering he endured.
behavior: The Caucasian Eagle is relentless and unwavering in its task. It is driven by the will of Zeus to inflict constant pain and suffering upon Prometheus. Its behavior is purely predatory and serves as a constant reminder of Prometheus's transgression. It displays no compassion or mercy, acting as a mere instrument of divine retribution.
weaknesses: Can be killed by a powerful hero

keyMyths.title: The Punishment of Prometheus
keyMyths.description: The Caucasian Eagle is central to the myth of Prometheus's punishment, where it daily devours his liver as retribution for giving fire to humanity. This act highlights Zeus's wrath and the Titan's enduring suffering. Heracles eventually slays the eagle, freeing Prometheus.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: An early Greek poem detailing the origins of the gods and the cosmos, including the story of Prometheus and the Caucasian Eagle.
sources.text: An early Greek poem detailing the origins of the gods and the cosmos, including the story of Prometheus and the Caucasian Eagle.

sources.author: Aeschylus
sources.description: A Greek tragedy focused on the plight of Prometheus, featuring the Caucasian Eagle as a symbol of Zeus's tyranny.
